title: Future Paths for Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Future Paths for Evangelion             Quote Post by facespace » Sun Jul 11, 2010 3:27 pm So when all the rebuilds are done and out of blu ray and dvd, where do you think Gainax will take their series next? In another 10 to 15 years do you see them rebuilding the series again? Release new movies? Where do you think Eva will go?

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Jul 11, 2010 3:45 pm In 10-15 years I'm sure there will be some fancy updated format, so they'll probably go back to the masters of the series & to rebuild and refurbish that shit for release in supradimensional 5-D holographic ultra-surround sound or something. This is, of course, assuming that 3.0/4.0 are released between now and then. Though I am kinda surprised that Gainax hasn't decided to capitalize on another NGE/EoE release for blu-rei, despite the fact that the original series probably wouldn't benefit much from a BD treatment.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Jul 11, 2010 4:28 pm Its probably just one of the blind spots companies have some times where they cant see a good idea staring them right in the face.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Jul 11, 2010 4:39 pm I really doubt that Evangelion will be visited again... No, wait... why not do an Evangelion series based upon the Manga version, which was somewhat different, yet similar, than the 1995 TV series? Face it, Evangelion is a money maker.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Jul 11, 2010 5:05 pm I would love to see the original series reanimated in high quality. Keep the characters and the story the same, but update the visuals. Call it a cash-in if you want, but I think preserving the story and essence of the original work while overhauling its sometimes-dated appearance would be a pretty fantastic way to give the original NGE a new leash on life. And it's not as though they'd have to worry about whether enough people would buy it to make it commercially viable. It has the Evangelion name on it, they'll make money from it :P

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 11, 2010 5:08 pm esselfortium wrote: Keep the characters and the story the same, but update the visuals. Call it a cash-in if you want

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Jul 11, 2010 5:10 pm I think it'd be a bit much if there were too many more "Re____ of Evangelion" projects (this isn't a Rebuild jab, but just..the naming convention for Eva updates/spinoffs is getting a bit monotonous at this point), but yeah. I'd gladly buy it, too.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Jul 11, 2010 5:24 pm esselfortium wrote: I would love to see the original series reanimated in high quality. Keep the characters and the story the same, but update the visuals. Call it a cash-in if you want, but I think preserving the story and essence of the original work while overhauling its sometimes-dated appearance would be a pretty fantastic way to give the original NGE a new leash on life. And it's not as though they'd have to worry about whether enough people would buy it to make it commercially viable. It has the Evangelion name on it, they'll make money from it :P

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sun Jul 11, 2010 5:40 pm They will finally do a sequel that tells what happens after people return from the LCL. Probably something like this: thewayneiac wrote: Ok, here's my idea for a sequel: Two or three years have passed and many people have returned. Like a snowball rolling downhill, the more time passes, the faster people return. (Most of the important characters are around when the show starts.) Enough people have returned that population centers have been reestablished, and vital services restored. Then, bad things start to happen, when beings resembling Angels start showing up and stomping the fragile new cities. Since we know there are no more Angels, just what can these things be? It seems that this show's bad guys, returned Seele members, have found that while being reembodied they have gained the ability to manipulate their A.T. fields at will and change their shapes. The bad guys can turn into angels. Fortunately, our heros discover that they have the same ability; they can turn into EVAs. (The original EVAs are obviously not available.) Shinji discovers this ability first, after a ghostly visit from Rei, who shows up only occasionally to offer him advice and encouragement. Things have been going reasonably well between Shinji and Asuka, but sour temporarily when she hears that he has been visited by Rei. ("But, it wasn't like that!!") Eventually she realizes that there is nothing going on between Shinji and the God-like Rei. (Whenever Asuka gets too obnoxious, Shinji jokingly threatens to take Misato up on her offer from EOE, though Asuka doesn't take this seriously, because Misato is happy with the reembodied Kaji.) Hmmm...., this sounds like 3-5 episodes already. For about the next 18-20 episodes Shinji, Asuka, and Toji fight another Seele member turned Angel every other episode or so. (Toji doesn't figure out how to re-grow his leg until after his first battle.) With 2-3 episodes left to go, they finally discover that there is a spy among them: Keel has manipulated his A.T. field to disguise himself as Gendo; then the real Gendo comes back and denounces him. At the end of ep. 25, Keel turns into the toughest Angel of them all, but all three pilots and Gendo all turn into EVAs and defeat him. Gendo patches things up with Shinji, (and with Ritsuko, after all, Yui is unavailable.), and everyone finally gets to live happily ever after. The last shot is a ghostly vision of Rei happliy watching over them. Note: Kensuke provides comedy relief throughout the series by constantly bitching because he can't turn into an EVA. As Joel would say: "What do you think, sirs?"

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Jul 11, 2010 5:58 pm Old but epic.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Jul 11, 2010 6:18 pm Once Rebuild is done then maybe they will give the series a rest. But seeing how the series is a money maker then they will still make new DVD releases for the series (Look like at Dragon Ball for example where the anime ended in 1997 but new DVD's are still keep on getting made). If the live action movie ever gets made then we hope for a series of live action movies like three or four movies.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Jul 11, 2010 6:42 pm Even more AU spinoffs.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sun Jul 11, 2010 7:01 pm They could make a anime spin off based on Angelic Days maybe.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sun Jul 11, 2010 7:08 pm I wish they wouldn’t, but I could easily see this happening.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Sun Jul 11, 2010 7:35 pm If it does, it better not share the same hideous ass art style as the actual manga or...they can just adapt the SIRP manga into an anime as well or something

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Sun Jul 11, 2010 7:39 pm Two words: animate RE-TAKE. If I could finance it, I swear to ever deity that I would . And then I'd start on my "classic fics" list. Oh, God help me If I ever gain wealth...

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sun Jul 11, 2010 7:44 pm AngelNo13Bardiel wrote: Two words: animate RE-TAKE. If I could finance it, I swear to ever deity that I would . And then I'd start on my "classic fics" list. Oh, God help me If I ever gain wealth...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Jul 11, 2010 8:27 pm thewayneiac wrote: They will finally do a sequel that tells what happens after people return from the LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Jul 11, 2010 10:48 pm Anima OVA. Ideally with a MAJOR aesthetic overhaul.

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Sun Jul 11, 2010 11:16 pm chee wrote: Anima OVA. Ideally with a MAJOR aesthetic overhaul.

Article: Future Paths for Evangelion
 centers have been reestablished, and vital services restored. Then, bad things start to happen, when beings resembling Angels start showing up and stomping the fragile new cities. Since we know there are no more Angels, just what can these things be? It seems that this show's bad guys, returned Seele members, have found that while being reembodied they have gained the ability to manipulate their A.T. fields at will and change their shapes. The bad guys can turn into angels. Fortunately, our heros discover that they have the same ability; they can turn into EVAs. (The original EVAs are obviously not available.) Shinji discovers this ability first, after a ghostly visit from Rei, who shows up only occasionally to offer him advice and encouragement. Things have been going reasonably well between Shinji and Asuka, but sour temporarily when she hears that he has been visited by Rei. ("But, it wasn't like that!!") Eventually she realizes that there is nothing going on between Shinji and the God-like Rei. (Whenever Asuka gets too obnoxious, Shinji jokingly threatens to take Misato up on her offer from EOE, though Asuka doesn't take this seriously, because Misato is happy with the reembodied Kaji.) Hmmm...., this sounds like 3-5 episodes already. For about the next 18-20 episodes Shinji, Asuka, and Toji fight another Seele member turned Angel every other episode or so. (Toji doesn't figure out how to re-grow his leg until after his first battle.) With 2-3 episodes left to go, they finally discover that there is a spy among them: Keel has manipulated his A.T. field to disguise himself as Gendo; then the real Gendo comes back and denounces him. At the end of ep. 25, Keel turns into the toughest Angel of them all, but all three pilots and Gendo all turn into EVAs and defeat him.
